name: Qwen3.7-Plus
archDisplay: MoE, exact expert configuration undisclosed; multimodal agent variant built on the Qwen3.7-Max backbone
note: Cheaper, multimodal (vision + video input) sibling of Qwen3.7-Max released ~2 weeks later on the Bailian/Model Studio platform; adds GUI grounding and screen/mobile agent capability (ScreenSpot Pro, AndroidWorld) on top of the Max agent backbone. Text output only (no image/video generation). Preceded by a preview from ~May 14, 2026.

results.notes: Terminal Bench 2.0-Terminus, outperforming DeepSeek-V4-Pro Max (67.9) and Gemini-3.1 Pro (63.5) per vendor comparison
SWE-Bench Verified, vendor-stated (Qwen's own launch benchmark table, run with thinking disabled). Qwen3.7-Plus trails Claude Opus 4.6 Max (80.8) and DeepSeek V4-Pro Max (80.6) on this metric.
OSWorld-Verified (desktop-GUI computer-use), vendor-stated on Qwen's own harness with thinking disabled; trails GPT-5.4 (75.0) on this metric per the same launch table.
Humanity's Last Exam, official Qwen launch blog, 'STEM & Reasoning' table (Qwen3.7-Plus column).
Official Qwen launch blog, 'STEM & Reasoning' table.
Official Qwen launch blog, 'General Capability' table.

Official Qwen launch blog, 'General Capability' table; recorded under this corpus's dedicated 'mmlu-redux' slug (not conflated with base MMLU, which was not separately reported for this model in this table).
SuperGPQA, official Qwen launch blog, 'General Capability' table.
Official Qwen launch blog, 'General Capability' table.
Official Qwen launch blog, 'General Capability' table; reported as a separate row from IFEval.

Official Qwen launch blog, 'Multimodal Benchmarks / Multimodal Reasoning' table.

Reported as 'CharXiv(RQ)' (reasoning-question subset) = '85.9 / 84.4' (the table's footnote states these are 'with CI / without CI' but does not define CI); 85.9 (the first-listed value) used as the primary figure here, consistent with how other Qwen-family models in this corpus record the CharXiv RQ subset under this slug.

Reported as 'VideoMME (w/ sub.)' in the official table's Video Understanding section, consistent with how several other Qwen-family models in this corpus record VideoMME-with-subtitles under this slug.

Atlas Cloud independently tested Qwen3.7-Plus, Qwen3.7-Max, and Qwen3.6-Plus on the actual 15-problem AIME 2025 I set, answers verified against AoPS/Areteem. Qwen3.7-Plus: 14/15 (93.3%) thinking-on, 12/15 (80.0%) thinking-off. No official Qwen or other independent-aggregator AIME score exists for this model, so this small-n third-party test is the only found evidence; flagged as such in the entry's own notes.

verificationNotes:
Confirmed real via multiple independent sources (MarkTechPost, VentureBeat headline citing the exact $0.4/$1.6 pricing, digitalapplied, buildfastwithai) and the official Alibaba Cloud Model Studio pricing page, which lists 'Qwen3.7-Plus' with the identical $0.40/$1.60 (0-256K tier) and a second $1.20/$4.80 tier for 256K-1M tokens -- confirming both the model's existence and its exact base pricing. GA date has minor variance (June 1-3, 2026 across sources); used June 2, 2026 (MarkTechPost publish date, closest to consensus). Pricing figure used is the base (0-256K) tier; a higher tier applies above 256K input tokens per the official pricing page. Architecture undisclosed across all sources, consistent with Qwen3.7-Max. Benchmark scores (GPQA Diamond 90.3, SWE-bench Pro ~57.6) directly confirmed via digitalapplied fetch; Terminal-Bench 70.3 corroborated by both the original VentureBeat-summarized search result and a direct buildfastwithai fetch. GUI/mobile-agent scores (ScreenSpot Pro 79.0, AndroidWorld 81.0, MRCR-v2 91.7) have no matching benchmark slug in the valid list and were omitted rather than misfiled under an existing slug.

2026-07-16 core-benchmark gap sweep (mmlu, math, aime, humaneval, mmmu): checked the official Qwen launch blog (qwen.ai/blog?id=qwen3.7-plus), independent aggregators (artificialanalysis.ai, llm-stats.com, benchlm.ai including its dedicated per-benchmark leaderboards, vals.ai), and the sibling Qwen3.7-Max entry already in this corpus. Base MMLU, MATH-500, HumanEval, and base MMMU (non-Pro) were not found for Qwen3.7-Plus anywhere: benchlm.ai's own MMLU, MATH-500, HumanEval, and MMMU leaderboards (which explicitly flag MMLU/HumanEval as saturated legacy benchmarks "excluded from the scoring formula") list no Qwen3.7 model at all. Qwen's own launch table substitutes MMLU-Pro (88.5) and MMLU-Redux (94.5) for base MMLU, HMMT/IMOAnswerBench/Apex for MATH-500, LiveCodeBench (89.6) for HumanEval, and MMMU-Pro (79) for base MMMU -- all already recorded in this file under their correct slugs. Sibling Qwen3.7-Max shows the identical substitution pattern (no base mmlu/math/humaneval/mmmu entries either), corroborating this is a deliberate vendor benchmark-suite choice for the 3.7 generation, not a data gap. mmlu, math, humaneval, and mmmu are therefore omitted as no-evidence. aime was resolved differently: added a 93.3% score (14/15, AIME 2025 I, thinking enabled) from Atlas Cloud's independently-run test (atlascloud.ai, published 2026-06-04), which evaluated Qwen3.7-Plus against the real AIME 2025 I problem set with answers verified against AoPS/Areteem; with thinking disabled the same test scored 80.0% (12/15). This is a small-sample (15-question) third-party test rather than a large-scale official result -- flagged as such in the entry's notes -- since no official Qwen AIME score or independent-aggregator AIME score exists for this model.

2026-07-17 SECOND-PASS deep gap sweep (mmlu, math, humaneval), specifically targeting source types NOT covered by the 2026-07-16 sweep (which had already ruled these out via the official Qwen blog, artificialanalysis.ai, llm-stats.com, benchlm.ai's per-benchmark leaderboards, vals.ai, and the Qwen3.7-Max sibling entry). This pass checked 6 new source categories:

(1) Stanford HELM -- directly browsed HELM Classic (crfm.stanford.edu/helm/classic, which has dedicated MMLU/MATH/HumanEval scenario tabs), HELM Lite (crfm.stanford.edu/helm/lite, full 91-model roster inspected), and HELM Capabilities/Core-Scenarios (MMLU-Pro-based). None of the three HELM leaderboards include any Qwen3.7-generation model at all -- the newest Qwen entries anywhere in HELM are Qwen2.5 Instruct Turbo (7B/72B) in Lite and Qwen3 235B A22B / Qwen3-Next 80B A3B in Capabilities. HELM has simply never run this (very recent, closed, API-only) model. Ruled out.

(2) arXiv/Semantic Scholar baseline citations -- multiple targeted searches ("Qwen3.7-Plus MMLU arxiv", "Qwen3.7-Plus MATH benchmark site:arxiv.org", "arxiv.org Qwen3.7-Plus table baseline", semantic-scholar-style queries) turned up only papers about earlier Qwen versions (Qwen3, Qwen2.5-Math, Qwen3.5-Omni, Qwen3-VL, etc.) -- none use Qwen3.7-Plus as a baseline. Expected: the model is ~6 weeks old (released 2026-06-02) and closed/API-only, so no paper has had time to benchmark it independently. Ruled out.

(3) GitHub eval-harness run logs -- searched for lm-evaluation-harness results, results.json-style commits, and general GitHub eval logs referencing qwen3.7-plus/qwen3_7_plus. Found only a QwenLM/qwen-code multimodal-support issue and generic Qwen3 (non-3.7) harness discussions; no committed eval run against this exact model. Ruled out.

(4) Other leaderboards/aggregators -- checked OpenRouter's model page (openrouter.ai/qwen/qwen3.7-plus: benchmark section present but populated with no MMLU/MATH/HumanEval figures), LiveBench.ai (no retrievable data on this model), MathArena.ai (browsed live; its BrokenArXiv/AIME/HMMT leaderboards list Qwen3.6-35B and Qwen3.5-2B but no Qwen3.7 model at all -- and MathArena evaluates fresh competition problems, not the classic MATH benchmark, so it would not resolve this slug even if present), and Scale AI SEAL / PapersWithCode (no hits). Ruled out.

(5) Non-English regional sources -- checked Chinese aggregator qianwenai.com's Qwen3.7-Plus model page (capability/pricing description only, zero benchmark numbers) and OpenCompass's official rank.opencompass.org.cn leaderboard (browsed live; its "官方评测榜" dated 2026-05-13 is a snapshot from before Qwen3.7-Plus's 2026-06-02 release and tops out at Qwen3.6-Max-Preview/Qwen3.6-27B -- no Qwen3.7 generation model listed at all). Korean- and Japanese-language searches ("벤치마크 MMLU", "ベンチマーク MMLU 数学") surfaced only the already-known Atlas Cloud AIME re-reporting and one blog (aimadetools.com) that explicitly *extrapolates* an unreported "~88.4%" MMLU-Pro estimate from a stated 1.2-point Max/Plus gap -- confirmed via direct fetch that this is the site's own inference, not a reported score, and was correctly excluded. Ruled out.

(6) Sibling-model comparison tables -- fetched BenchLM.ai's dedicated Qwen3.7-Max model page (benchlm.ai/models/qwen3-7-max), which is far more exhaustive than the per-benchmark leaderboards checked in the prior round: it lists ~57 individual benchmark results for Qwen3.7-Max across agentic/coding/reasoning/knowledge/math/multilingual/instruction-following/multimodal categories. Even this exhaustive sibling page has zero rows for base MMLU, MATH, or HumanEval (it substitutes MMLU-Pro/MMLU-Redux/MMMLU, HMMT/IMOAnswerBench/Apex, and LiveCodeBench respectively -- the identical substitution pattern already noted for Plus) and contains no comparison row for Qwen3.7-Plus itself. Also fetched aimadetools.com's dedicated Max-vs-Plus comparison table (six paired metrics: Intelligence Index, Terminal-Bench Hard, CritPt, SWE-bench Verified, MMLU-Pro, GPQA Diamond) -- again MMLU-Pro only, no base MMLU/MATH/HumanEval for either model. Ruled out.

Conclusion: this is now a well-corroborated, deliberate reporting gap rather than a research gap -- confirmed independently through HELM's non-coverage, arXiv's non-coverage, GitHub's non-coverage, three leaderboard aggregators' non-coverage, two non-English regional sources' non-coverage, and the sibling Qwen3.7-Max's own most-exhaustive-available benchmark page substituting the same alternate metrics. mmlu, math, and humaneval remain omitted as no-evidence; no new results added in this pass.
